summary: Category-aware scorer filtering eliminates false positives instead of modifying plugin skills
context: Phase 19 tuning cycle analyzed 41 eval failures. 35 were expected negatives (violation scenarios correctly not exhibiting behaviors). 6 were false positives where sequencing and workflow-completeness scorers triggered on scenarios outside their category scope.
rationale: The failures were scorer precision issues, not plugin instruction gaps. Fixing scorers to respect scenario categories (only checking sequencing/completeness for the workflow matching the scenario's declared category) is more correct than warping plugin instructions to avoid triggering cross-category checks.

alternatives.option: Modify plugin skill descriptions to avoid triggering cross-category scorer matches
alternatives.reason_rejected: Would degrade skill quality to work around scorer imprecision — wrong layer to fix

alternatives.option: Lower scorer thresholds to tolerate false positives
alternatives.reason_rejected: Masks real failures, reduces eval sensitivity
